quote: Ran his pitch count only to 68 in his final rehab start; Red Sox say they'll "continue to build up his pitch count" and hadn't locked starter vs reliever — likely on a restriction in his first game back.
Brewers easing him in — threw 74 pitches last time with plans to "crack the 80-pitch marker" this start.
On a managed build-up — 75 then 85 pitches in back-to-back starts, ~5 IP, still used as a swingman.
NOT a limit this year — coach Kyle Snyder called ~170 innings "very reasonable" for 2026 after capping him at 6 IP max all 2025. Logged so you don't mistake him for an under.
